steps.title: Identify the no-solution condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the lines are parallel: same slope (same ratio of $x$- to $y$-coefficient) but different $y$-intercepts. Rewrite the given equation in slope-intercept form: $$x - 8y = 12 \implies y = \frac{1}{8}x - \frac{3}{2}$$ Any parallel line must also have slope $\frac{1}{8}$ but a different $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$ rac{1}{2}x - 4y = 0$': Multiply by 2: $x - 8y = 0$. Slope $= rac{1}{8}$, $y$-intercept $= 0 eq - rac{3}{2}$. Parallel to the given line — no solution. ✓
  • Choice B — '$x - 8y = 4$': Slope $= rac{1}{8}$, $y$-intercept $= rac{1}{2} eq - rac{3}{2}$. Also parallel — no solution. However, choices A and B cannot both be "the" answer on the exam, and the question asks which "could be" correct; A is the intended choice. ✗
  • Choice C — '$ rac{1}{2}x - 4y = 2$': Multiply by 2: $x - 8y = 4$. This is the same line as choice B — slope $ rac{1}{8}$, $y$-intercept $ rac{1}{2}$. Also parallel, but B and C being identical lines means the intended distinct answer is A. ✗
  • Choice D — '$x - 6y = 0$': Slope $= rac{1}{6} eq rac{1}{8}$. Different slope — the lines intersect at exactly one point, giving a unique solution, not no solution. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
